We also recommended and Municipal Accountant and Treasurer agreed to conduct regular reconciliations of the RPT and SET receivable accounts based on the RPTAR/Taxpayer’s Index Card balances and to implement the necessary adjustments to ensure the accuracy and validity of recorded receivables.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds accounts by following the PAJE outlined below:

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Engineer, GSO and Municipal Accountant agreed to the following:
Collaborate to develop a systematic approach for meeting the accounting and reporting requirements outlined in COA Circular No. 2015-008; and.
Attend or organize training sessions or workshops, in coordination with DILG Regional Office VII, to stay updated on LRAMS changes and ensure compliance with the road network reporting guidelines.

We recommended, and the Public Employment Service Office agreed, to expedite the utilization of the remaining ₱59,349.00 balance for the livelihood program to fully realize the objectives of the financial assistance.

We recommended and Management, through the BAC, agreed to require the contractor to provide a Performance Security for the remaining period until the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance, in compliance with Sections 39.3 and 62.2.2 of the RIRR of RA 9184. This will protect the Municipality from incurring unnecessary costs due to damage caused by the use of inferior quality materials, if such will be the case.

We recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee agreed to the following courses of actions:
In coordination with the end-user unit representative, conduct an evaluation of the practicality, economy, and advantages of using a Framework Agreement, and adopt it to update procurement processes for recurring needs;
Attend training sessions, in coordination with GPPB-recognized trainers, to stay updated on changes in procurement regulations, particularly regarding the implementation of Framework Agreements, as per GPPB guidelines; and
Ensure that purchase orders specify the required delivery terms, so that the responsible party can be easily identified in case of issues during transportation and delivery of goods.

We recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee agreed to the following:
Submit the APPs, PMRs, and APCPI results, along with the certification signed by the HoPE, via email to the GPPB within the prescribed deadline, as per GPPB Circular No. 02-2020; and
Prepare the APCPI results using the form or tool prescribed by the GPPB.

We recommended and the Market Supervisor-Designate agreed to facilitate the execution of lease contracts with 201 market stallholders to establish clear, enforceable agreements that protect the interests of both parties and ensure efficient market operations.

We recommended and Management, in coordination with the Human Resource Management Officer, agreed to revive the plan to designate an IAS Officer and establish the IASO in accordance with the guidelines set forth in LBC 153.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation, and Management, through the MDRRMO, agreed to maximize the utilization of the 70 per cent Mitigation Fund under LDRRMF, in line with its intended purpose. This will help fully achieve the Municipality’s objective of strengthening disaster preparedness and response capabilities, ultimately better protecting constituents from the impacts of disasters.
We further recommended, and the MDRRMO agreed, to present the status report of LDRRMFIP PPAs during LDRRMC meetings to identify and address issues, ensuring the timely execution of these PPAs through necessary corrective actions.

observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan has enacted ordinance #2016-50 for the implementation of the Balamban Community Housing Project. The committee made under ordinance met and came up with the resolution to request the Provincial Appraisal Committee to Conduct an ocular inspection for appraisal of all aggregate lots. They found that the cost of the lots would possibly be too expensive for employees to avail of. They intend to refer to COA as to the possibility of the lot to be sold as a whole.
observations.reason: The committee found that the cost of the lots would possibly be too expensive for employees to avail of. They intend to refer to COA as to the possibility of the lot to be sold as a whole.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accounting and Treasurer’s Offices reconcile their property records and reports periodically. Further, the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer and other offices concerned are enjoined to come up with the appropriate costing of the housings lots in line with the purpose as an aide to the Sangguniang Bayan in the formulation of an ordinance prescribing the policies or guidelines in the disposition, acquisition, cost recovery and other pertinent matters in the implementation of Balamban Community Housing Projects.
observations.managementAction: The accounting and treasury Offices are reconciling their property records and reports so that all inventory reports so that inventory of lots for sale would be prepared with inventory reports like the other inventory reports, as this caused the variance between booked inventories & actual per count.
observations.reason: Further verification, a variance totaling P24,464,533.24 is still noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Inventory Committee to complete the Report of Physical Count of PPE and reconcile the results with the accounting records and submit a copy of the report to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: The municipal treasurer submitted the complete Report on the Physical Count of PPE on 8/11/2017 by Ms. Karen M. Dumdum.
observations.reason: Further verification, a variance totaling P24,464,533.24 is still noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 be filled up immediately by the Municipality to properly institutionalize the organization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.
observations.managementAction: The municipality is currently institutionalizing the organization of the LDRRMO. Mr. Genito Candia was appointed last March 12, 2017 and he is handling the operations and warning sections. Hopefully the remaining 2 positions would be filled up this year. One of the remaining position was filled up by Genito Candila with SG18.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Inventory Committee to complete the Report of Physical Count of PPE and reconcile the results with the accounting records and submit a copy of the report to the audit team.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommend that the Local School Board hasten the implementation of the priority programs and projects to achieve the purpose of the fund.

Moreover, the members of the Local School Board are enjoined to get a copy of the Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 issued by DepEd, DBM and DILG and keep abreast with the revised guidelines on the use of the SEF for CY 2017.
We recommend that the current Local School Board revisit the provisions of the Section 98(b) and (c) of RA 7160 and reorganize the composition of the Municipal School Board.

We recommend that the positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 be filled up immediately by the Municipality to properly institutionalize the organization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.
We recommend that management procure common-use supplies directly from the Procurement Service of the Department of Budget and Management (PS-DBM).

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should submit the required authority from the SB for the Municipal Mayor to enter into a contract in behalf of the LGU to implement the projects totaling P 27,417,761.95.
observations.managementAction: All disbursements from lump sum appropriations are now supported with SB resolutions authorizing utilization from such appropriations. An SB resolution granting authority to the mayor to enter into a contract in behalf of the LGU is also required

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that a MOA requiring liquidation report be executed between the LGU and implementing agency prior to fund release.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant should certify that funds previously released to the requesting agency had been liquidated, verified and accounted for in the books. 

We further recommend that all disbursement for financial aid/assistance should be supported with complete documentation as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: All concerned offices as well as barangay captains, barangay treasurers were informed regarding this audit memorandum

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned job order workers be required to submit their individual accomplishment reports duly verified and signed by their immediate supervisors in adherence to Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and Section 1.2.1 of COA Circular 2012-001.  This is to ensure that the paid wages of the job order workers were actually rendered in accordance with their assigned tasks and their services were truly necessary.
observations.managementAction: Immediate implementation / preparation of the accomplishment reports duly verified by the dept. head/supervisors is done. These are attached to the payrolls to substantiate the necessity of the services of the job order workers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the requisitioning officer and BAC Chairman submit the lacking documents and a written explanation on non-compliance with the applicable provisions of EO 49 and the Procurement Law.  Henceforth, the Municipal Health Officer and the BAC should see to it that all provisions of EO 49 and the Revised IRR of RA 9184 are faithfully complied with in order not to invalidate their procurement.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the lacking documents and follow the provisions of RA 9184 and EO 49 for the purchase of drug products.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the practice of charging to the 20% development fund expenses that do not partake the nature of investment / capital expenditures and to appropriate and utilize the 20% Development Fund in accordance with the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1, to ensure optimum utilization of the fund on projects that would help achieve desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes.
observations.managementAction: Stopped the charging of the medicines, medical and dental supplies to the 20% Devt. Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC conduct public bidding by consolidating the Purchase Requisitions to avail of volume discounts and to obtain the most advantageous price.
observations.managementAction: Purchase Requests of the same class were  consolidated and are subjected to competitive  public bidding

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO) and the Municipal Accountant should ensure that the payroll on the computation of leave credits (monetization) is supported or attached with all the required documents pursuant to the pertinent CSC and COA Rules and Regulations.

They are further enjoined to submit all the supporting documents on the monetization paid in CY 2015, to avoid issuance of the Notice of Suspension.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO and accountant ensured that the payroll for the monetization of leave credits is supported with all the required documents pursuant to pertinent CSC and COA rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC immediately submit the lacking documents for post-audit to complete the evaluation of the contracts/transactions, as to validity and compliance to the requirements of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: BAC submitted the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the BAC undertake all procurement activities of the municipality including the recommendation on the mode of procurement.

	The Municipal Accountant should ensure that the subject procurement thru cash advances be supported with evidence of BAC action and the appropriate supporting documents on the mode of procurement recommended by the BAC.
observations.managementAction: Procurement of food supplies for the Supplemental Feeding Program was public bidded in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should require the BAC Secretariat to submit all the necessary/required documents and other evidences to support the honorarium claims and attach them to the disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat was required to submit all the documents to support the claims of the honoraria and attached them to the disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Accounting Department to ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments made in accordance with the Revised Withholding Tax Table and the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Required the Accounting Department to deduct withholding taxes from BAC honoraria

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board strictly comply with the provisions of Section 272 of Republic Act (RA) 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular Nos. 1 s. 1988 and 01-A s. 2000.  Expenses not within the purposes of the Special Education Fund (SEF) should charged to the appropriations of the General Fund or to DepEd funds.
observations.managementAction: In formulating the 2016 budget for the SEF, the Local School Board should follow the provisions stated in this audit memorandum

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the personnel in-charge in the preparation of the payroll should compute the Subsistence and Laundry allowances of PHWs pursuant to DOH-DBM Joint Circular No 1, s. 2012.

The Municipal Accountant is also enjoined to review the computation of the allowances to ensure correctness of the claim.
observations.managementAction: The DOH – DBM joint circular no. 1 s. 2012 should be followed in the preparation of the payroll for the subsistence and laundry allowances of the PHWs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should hasten the implementation of mitigation projects under the LDRRMF to achieve the purpose of the fund.  LGU officials must ensure that the LDRRM Plan and Fund Investment Plan are integrated into their Local Development Plan and the Monthly Report on the Sources and Mitigation of the DRRMF be submitted to the Auditor promptly.
observations.managementAction: Hastened the implementation of mitigation projects. The LDRRM plan is integrated into the Annual Investment Program. Preparation of the monthly report of the PRRMF is regularly done.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that proper monitoring of project expenses be implemented to prevent irregular expenditures.

Further, the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Engineer are enjoined to submit a written explanation why expenditures were charged to already completed projects.
observations.managementAction: Monitored project expenses so that they would be properly charged.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant deduct the equivalent amount of the absences incurred on regular sessions from the RATA of the concerned elective officials in conformity with Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated on May 15, 2013. 

Furthermore, the SB Secretary should notify the Sangguniang Bayan the mandated minimum number of regular sessions in conformity to Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: Follow the provisions of LBC No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 in the preparation of the RATA Payroll. Deduct the overpay from the current payrolls.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the twenty-nine (29) laborers to refund immediately the excess payment they received.  Otherwise, municipal officials/employees participating in the processing, approving and paying the claim shall be held accountable therefore.
observations.managementAction: Required the 29 laborers to refund the excess payment

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that all concerned officials and employees be directed to refund the amount in excess of the P5,000.00 of PEI received, otherwise, all those who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable.

Henceforth, in payment of personnel incentives and similar allowances strict compliance with the provisions of applicable Executive Order as may be implemented by pertinent Budget Circular and other pertinent laws is enjoined to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: The municipality appealed the disallowance of the PEI at the COA Regional Offices then to the COA Central Office last January 14, 2015.
observations.reason: Currently awaiting the decision of COA Central Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall assign an accounting staff to trace back the account balance to identify the employee from whom the amount was deducted and cause the corresponding remittance to GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The SEF bookkeeper is tasked to trace back the Due to GSIS account under the SEF books.
observations.reason: Tracing of this account is on-going and necessary adjustment would be made once prior years’ records are seen.

observations.recommendation: The head of the agency should enforce collection of said disallowances and charges, considering that they have already become final and executor due to the length of time that they remained unsettled.
observations.managementAction: The municipal treasurer sent collection/demand letters to two persons with material amounts of disallowances namely Pedro Rebucias and Phanuel Senoron.
observations.reason: As of 12/31/16 the balance of the disallowance/charges which have been unsettled for 11 years has been reduced to P180,591.25.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan should enact a Municipal Ordinance prescribing all policies pertinent to the project to include disposition of home-lots, cost recovery and state management as provided under paragraph 1.8 of Article of Memorandum of Agreement.
